svg.text: Quickselect vs. Bounded Heap
Quickselect (offline)
Pick a pivot, partition into less / equal / greater.
Recurse only into the side containing the k-th index.
Average O(n); worst O(n²) without good pivot choice.
Median-of-medians for guaranteed O(n) — rarely needed.
In-place: O(1) extra memory.
Returns only the k-th value; surrounding
partition gives the top-k as a side effect.
Good when: full array fits in memory,
average case is acceptable, no streaming.
Hoare partition or Lomuto — Hoare is
faster but trickier to get right.
Pitfall: bad pivot on sorted input
⇒ O(n²). Randomise pivot.
Bounded heap (streaming)
Min-heap of size k for "k largest".
For each x: push; if size > k pop.
Top is the smallest of the kept items —
the next eviction candidate.
Time: O(n log k) deterministic.
Space: O(k).
Returns all k items in arbitrary order.
Drain to get them sorted.
Good when: stream, k « n, worst-case
guarantees matter, follow-up scaling.
Polarity check: writing "max-heap"
for "k largest" is the most common bug.
Pitfall: forgetting the size cap
⇒ O(n log n), no win over sort.
